text.title: Latency vs Quality Decision Grid Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure from call-site inventory to a live, monitored decision grid.

step.name: inventory-call-sites
step.action: Enumerate every LLM call site: route, current model, current tactic, UX surface, owner. Deduplicate identical sites.

step.name: measure-baseline
step.input: Last 7 days of telemetry + current eval scores.
step.action: Record p50, p95 latency, quality score, cost-per-call per site.
step.decision-gate: Any column missing → fix instrumentation before continuing.

step.name: propose-grid
step.input: Baseline + UX guidelines + candidate-model cost table.
step.action: For each site, propose (latency_p50_ms, latency_p95_ms, quality_floor, model, tactic). Justify each row in ≤2 sentences. Note expected delta vs baseline.
step.decision-gate: If any row's proposed quality_floor < current baseline → require sign-off from site owner.

step.name: abx-rollout
step.action: Wire each model swap behind a flag; run AB test (e.g. 10% traffic) for ≥48h; record observed latency + quality + cost deltas.
step.decision-gate: If observed regression >5% on either axis, abort and revert (rule r4).

step.name: wire-routing
step.action: Emit routing config consumed by the runtime (e.g. router.yaml). Pin grid.json + last_reviewed timestamp.
step.decision-gate: Validator script must pass before merge.

step.name: monitor
step.action: Dashboard shows per-site p50/p95/quality/cost. Alert on 15-min SLO breach. Quarterly review re-runs the procedure.
step.output: dashboard URL + alert rules + quarterly cadence on calendar.
step.decision-gate: If alert fires for the same site twice in a quarter, re-run procedure from step 2 for that site.
